review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Digital Unit Suffers Under Toxic Leadership of CDO Charu Mathur
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: High Pay for New Joiners: The Digital Unit Offers Irrational Salaries but Beware of Mental Health Risks from Long-Term Stays
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Dysfunctional Culture in the Digital Unit

The Digital Unit under CDO is plagued by severe issues that create a toxic work environment. 

1. Abusive Leadership: The CDO is known to shout and throw objects at employees. Her impulsive behavior and erratic decisions play havoc with people’s careers, leading to a culture of fear and self-doubt among staff. Former employees and current vendors, if they choose to speak out, can easily verify these claims.

2. Lack of Accountability: Despite numerous complaints, no action has been taken against the CDO because she is backed by MD. This protection has allowed her to continue her detrimental practices unchecked.

3. Non-compliance and Mismanagement: The organization frequently experiences lapses and financial frauds due to poor decision-making. Instead of addressing these issues, efforts are made to hide them from the regulator. This indicates a deep-seated culture of non-compliance and mismanagement.

4. Delayed and Unfair Compensation: Appraisal payments are delayed until July, effectively denying payouts to employees who resign. Additionally, there is no compensation for accrued privilege leaves.

5. Ineffective HR Department: HR is practically non-existent, failing to support employees or address their grievances adequately.

6. Promotion Based on Favoritism: Success in the unit depends more on managing the CDO’s moods than on actual work skills. Employees are promoted for devising non-ideal plans rather than contributing to business growth.

7. Detrimental to Mental Health: The overall culture severely impacts mental health. Employees often feel isolated, but reaching out to former employees can provide support and reassurance that they are not alone in their experiences.

In conclusion, the toxic culture within the Digital Unit under CDO’s leadership not only hampers professional growth but also poses significant risks to employees’ mental well-being.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Work life balance. Can close branch at 6:15 pm if you are smart and can manage your business. Whereas in banks like Axis Bank, HDFC Bank or ICICI Bank 8 pm is routine close time.
2. Operations workload is very less as compared to HDFC Bank ,Axis Bank or ICICI Bank
3. Focus on selected products like LI GI Debit cards and A/C ,s . You have to achieve a minimum monthly target called MOP. your life is comfortable if you do your MOP month on month.
4. Chance of promotion in one year 
5. Interest rates are best in market. So acquring new deposits is easier.

4. Systems and processes are not very robust

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: If you've been in good companies earlier you'll regret joining IndusInd Bank after one year. Appraisal is around 3-4% inspite of 4/5 rating. Appraisal process is purposely done from August to August so that you get deprived of getting considered for appraisal when you join next company.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
While I am a seasoned professional who has got extensive work experience, I cannot think of a Positive Reason to work here. The worst part is that they make you sell Insurance whether you are in their Select Team or in their Exclusive Team. 

The only good thing is that they give you a designation as a Partner, although it doesn't mean anything.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Those who are keen on joining IndusInd  should first go through these points before making a decision. 

1. A lot of false promises, misinformation and lies are sold to candidates during the interview. A rosy picture is given. On joining the bank, you understand that it takes more than 2 months to get your issued laptop. As a Senior RM, you don't have a place to sit. There is no clarity on branch allotment. There is nothing right with this place. And you get paid peanuts for going through this own personal hell.  

2. The client book given to you does not have purity. Anyone from across the bank can pitch to any client. The only exception are those few RMs who are close to senior management and no one is allowed to touch their clientele. Unlike the bigger better banks, this one is a den of unprofessional conduct and lies. If you are a perfomer, they will extract and extract some more with the promises of bonuses and awards. Most of these either never come or they come with a huge delay. The reason for this is that their Select and Exclusive Teams have the highest attrition rates in the industry. Delaying incentives prevents people from leaving the organization in a hurry and allows the bank to extract some more.  

3. If you are in a habit of listening to street swear words and unparliamentary language gets you a kick then this is the bank for you. Everyone from the Zonal Head are case studies in Narcissism. Some of them need psychiatric help. Mind you no support will be given to you - even your laptop wont come on time, but there will be pressure to bring revenue. The day you bring your clients from your previous workplace everyone in the bank pounces on them to get business. As mentioned earlier - no purity of book and no ethics. Everyone says that the CEO Mr, Sobti personally tells people to work in this manner. 

4. Let me give you a small statistic. Of the 10 people who joined this place, 8 left in the first three months and most people would not remain beyond six months. If you are joining the bank you will notice that besides the senior people, the entire RM Team vintage will never be beyond 6 months. The credit goes to the environment in the bank and the resultant high attrition.
